Chief Complaint: Transferred from Honor Health Shea Medical Center for improvement in the
History of Present Illness: this is a very pleasant 87 year female who has known history of Parkinson
disease, hypertension, hyperthyroidism. Patient lives alone and recently sustained a ground level fall
leading to hospitalization where the workup found to have right periorbital ecchymosis/hematoma,
nontraumatic rhabdomyolysis, non ST-elevation MI secondary to fall and rhabdomyolysis. Patient had
extensive workup in the hospital including seen by Cardiology and Neurology. Patient was
subsequently seen by PT and oT they recommended acute rehab. Patient got transferred to
encompass Health on March 19. Patient seen in room this morning, daughter at the bedside. Per
daughter patient was pretty independent until year ago when she got diagnosed with a Parkinson
disease. Patient is still able to live independently.
Past Medical/ Surgical History: As outlined above

Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Scottsdale
"Given the patient's rehabilitation need and on going medical issues, this patient is best served in a
comprehensive inpatient rehabilitation setting with an interdisciplinary approach with at least 3 hours
of skilled therapies for at least 5 days per week. Considering all the information above, it is my best
judgement that this patient requires intensive therapy in an inpatient rehabilitation hospital due to
rehabilitation needs. This patient can reasonably be expected to participate in and benefit in an
interdisciplinary delivery of care under the direction of a rehabilitation or equally certified physician.
The goal is to return the patient to as close as possible to prior function."
